chapters.chapter_title: Chapter I: Preliminary
section_titles: Section 1: Short title, extent and commencement
Section 2: Definitions
Section 3: Construction of references
Section 4: Trial of offences under Bhartiya Nyaya Sanhita and other laws
Section 5: Saving
chapters.summary: This chapter provides the preliminary information about the Act, including its title, extent, and commencement. It also defines key terms and explains how the Act will be constructed.

chapters.chapter_title: Chapter II: Constitution of Criminal Courts and Offices
section_titles: Section 6: Classes of Criminal Courts
Section 7: Territorial divisions
Section 8: Court of Session
Section 9: Courts of Judicial Magistrates
Section 10: Chief Judicial Magistrate and Additional Chief Judicial Magistrate, etc.
chapters.summary: This chapter deals with the constitution of criminal courts and offices, including the establishment of courts of session, judicial magistrates, and executive magistrates.

chapters.chapter_title: Chapter III: Power of Courts
section_titles: Section 21: Courts by which offences are triable
Section 22: Sentences which High Courts and Sessions Judges may pass
Section 23: Sentences which Magistrates may pass
Section 24: Sentence of imprisonment in default of fine
Section 25: Sentence in cases of conviction of several offences at one trial
chapters.summary: This chapter explains the powers of courts, including which courts can try offenses and what sentences they can impose.

chapters.chapter_title: Chapter IV: Powers of Superior Officers of Police and Aid to the Magistrates and the Police
section_titles: Section 30: Powers of superior officers of police
Section 31: Public when to assist Magistrates and police
Section 32: Aid to person, other than police officer, executing warrant
Section 33: Public to give information of certain offences
Section 34: Duty of officers employed in connection with the affairs of a village to make certain report
chapters.summary: This chapter deals with the powers of superior officers of police and the aid they can provide to magistrates and the police.

chapters.chapter_title: Chapter V: Arrest of Persons
section_titles: Section 35: When police may arrest without warrant
Section 36: Procedure of arrest and duties of officer making arrest
Section 37: Designated Police Officer
Section 38: Right of arrested person to meet an advocate of his choice during interrogation
Section 39: Arrest on refusal to give name and residence
chapters.summary: This chapter explains the procedures for arresting persons, including when police can arrest without a warrant and the rights of the arrested person.

chapters.chapter_title: Chapter VI: Processes to Compel Appearance
section_titles: Section 63: Form of summons
Section 64: Summons how served
Section 65: Service of summons on corporate bodies, firms, and societies
Section 66: Service when persons summoned cannot be found
Section 67: Procedure when service cannot be effected as before provided
chapters.summary: This chapter deals with the processes used to compel the appearance of persons in court, including summons and warrants.

chapters.chapter_title: Chapter VII: Process to Compel the Production of Things
section_titles: Section 94: Summons to produce document or other thing
Section 95: Procedure as to letters and telegrams
Section 96: When search-warrant may be issued
Section 97: Search of place suspected to contain stolen property, forged documents, etc.
Section 98: Power to declare certain publications forfeited and to issue search-warrants for the same
chapters.summary: This chapter explains the processes used to compel the production of things, including search warrants and summons to produce documents.

chapters.chapter_title: Chapter VIII: Reciprocal Arrangements for Assistance in Certain Matters and Procedure for Attachment and Forfeiture of Property
section_titles: Section 111: Definitions
Section 112: Letter of request to competent authority for investigation in a country or place outside India
Section 113: Letter of request from a country or place outside India to a Court or an authority for investigation in India
Section 114: Assistance in securing transfer of persons
Section 115: Assistance in relation to orders of attachment or forfeiture of property
chapters.summary: This chapter deals with reciprocal arrangements for assistance in certain matters and the procedure for attachment and forfeiture of property.

chapters.chapter_title: Chapter IX: Security for Keeping the Peace and for Good Behaviour
section_titles: Section 125: Security for keeping the peace on conviction
Section 126: Security for keeping the peace in other cases
Section 127: Security for good behaviour from persons disseminating seditious matters
Section 128: Security for good behaviour from suspected persons
Section 129: Security for good behaviour from habitual offenders
chapters.summary: This chapter explains the provisions for security for keeping the peace and for good behaviour, including the conditions and procedures for imposing security.

chapters.chapter_title: Chapter X: Order for Maintenance of Wives, Children and Parents
section_titles: Section 144: Order for maintenance of wives, children and parents
Section 145: Procedure
Section 146: Alteration in allowance
Section 147: Enforcement of order of maintenance
chapters.summary: This chapter deals with the provisions for ordering maintenance for wives, children, and parents, including the procedure and enforcement of such orders.

chapters.chapter_title: Chapter XI: Maintenance of Public Order and Tranquillity
section_titles: Section 148: Dispersal of assembly by use of civil force
Section 149: Use of armed forces to disperse assembly
Section 150: Power of certain armed force officers to disperse assembly
Section 151: Protection against prosecution for acts done under sections 148, 149 and 150
Section 152: Conditional order for removal of nuisance
chapters.summary: This chapter explains the provisions for maintaining public order and tranquillity, including the dispersal of assemblies and the removal of nuisances.

chapters.chapter_title: Chapter XII: Preventive Action of the Police
section_titles: Section 168: Police to prevent cognizable offences
Section 169: Information of design to commit cognizable offences
Section 170: Arrest to prevent the commission of cognizable offences
Section 171: Prevention of injury to public property
Section 172: Persons bound to conform to lawful directions of police
chapters.summary: This chapter deals with the preventive actions that the police can take, including preventing cognizable offenses and protecting public property.

chapters.chapter_title: Chapter XIII: Information to the Police and Their Powers to Investigate
section_titles: Section 173: Information in cognizable cases
Section 174: Information as to non-cognizable cases and investigation of such cases
Section 175: Police officer's power to investigate cognizable case
Section 176: Procedure for investigation
Section 177: Report how submitted
chapters.summary: This chapter explains the provisions for information to the police and their powers to investigate, including the procedures for investigating cognizable and non-cognizable cases.

chapters.chapter_title: Chapter XIV: Jurisdiction of the Criminal Courts in Inquiries and Trials
section_titles: Section 197: Ordinary place of inquiry and trial
Section 198: Place of inquiry or trial
Section 199: Offence triable where act is done or consequence ensues
Section 200: Place of trial where act is an offence by reason of relation to other offence
Section 201: Place of trial in case of certain offences
chapters.summary: This chapter deals with the jurisdiction of criminal courts in inquiries and trials, including the ordinary place of inquiry and trial.

chapters.chapter_title: Chapter XV: Conditions Requisite for Initiation of Proceedings
section_titles: Section 210: Cognizance of offences by Magistrates
Section 211: Transfer on application of the accused
Section 212: Making over of cases to Magistrates
Section 213: Cognizance of offences by Courts of Session
Section 214: Additional Sessions Judges to try cases made over to them
chapters.summary: This chapter explains the conditions required for initiating proceedings, including cognizance of offenses by magistrates and courts of session.

chapters.chapter_title: Chapter XVI: Complaints to Magistrates
section_titles: Section 223: Examination of complainant
Section 224: Procedure by Magistrate not competent to take cognizance of the case
Section 225: Postponement of issue of process
Section 226: Dismissal of complaint
chapters.summary: This chapter deals with complaints to magistrates, including the examination of the complainant and the procedure for dismissing complaints.

chapters.chapter_title: Chapter XVII: Commencement of Proceedings Before Magistrates
section_titles: Section 227: Issue of process
Section 228: Magistrate may dispense with personal attendance of accused
Section 229: Special summons in cases of petty offence
Section 230: Supply to the accused of copy of police report and other documents
Section 231: Supply of copies of statements and documents to accused in other cases triable by Court of Session
chapters.summary: This chapter explains the commencement of proceedings before magistrates, including the issue of process and the supply of documents to the accused.

chapters.chapter_title: Chapter XVIII: The Charge
section_titles: Section 234: Contents of charge
Section 235: Particulars as to time, place and person
Section 236: When manner of committing offence must be stated
Section 237: Words in charge taken in sense of law under which offence is punishable
Section 238: Effect of errors
chapters.summary: This chapter deals with the charge, including its contents and the particulars that must be stated.

chapters.chapter_title: Chapter XIX: Trial Before a Court of Session
section_titles: Section 248: Trial to be conducted by Public Prosecutor
Section 249: Opening case for prosecution
Section 250: Discharge
Section 251: Framing of charge
Section 252: Conviction on plea of guilty
chapters.summary: This chapter explains the trial before a court of session, including the conduct of the trial by the public prosecutor and the framing of the charge.

section_titles: Section 261: Compliance with section 231
Section 262: When accused shall be discharged
Section 263: Framing of charge
Section 264: Conviction on plea of guilty

chapters.summary: This chapter deals with the trial of warrant-cases by magistrates, including the compliance with section 231 and the framing of the charge.
